title: My Mother at Sixty Six class 12 summary | My mother at 66 class 12 | explanation
description:
Kamala Das wrote "My Mother at Sixty-Six" carrying a quiet, devastating fear most of us recognize but rarely admit — that one ordinary car ride might be the last time we truly see our mother alive. This poem, studied in Class 12 CBSE Flamingo, captures the unbearable tension between a daughter's helplessness and her desperate optimism, weaving themes of aging, mortality, and a child's love into just a handful of lines. What makes Das's imagery so haunting is how she refuses to sentimentalize grief — she just shows you a pale face, a dozing mother, and a smile that has to carry everything words cannot.
